These wasps are known to be parasitoids, which means they lay their eggs inside the bodies of other insects. Volvulus inflatus is specifically a parasitoid of the caterpillars of certain moth species. The classification of Volvulus inflatus is further divided into the genus Volvulus and the species V. inflatus. This parasitic wasp is typically found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world.
